fabric-loot-api-v2.function: LootTable replaceLootTable(RegistryKey<LootTable> key, LootTable original, LootTableSource source);
fabric-loot-api-v2.description:
This event can be used to replace loot tables. If a loot table is replaced, the iteration will stop for that loot table
Deprecated: Please use REPLACE instead.

fabric-loot-api-v2.function: void modifyLootTable(RegistryKey<LootTable> key, LootTable.Builder tableBuilder, LootTableSource source);

fabric-loot-api-v2.description: Called when a loot table is loading to modify loot tables

fabric-command-api-v2.description: Callback for when client commands are registered to the dispatcher. To register some commands, you would register an event listener and implement the callback. See ClientCommandManager for more details and an example

fabric-command-api-v2.function: void register(CommandDispatcher<ServerCommandSource> dispatcher, CommandRegistryAccess registryAccess, CommandManager.RegistrationEnvironment environment);

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowElytraFlight(LivingEntity entity);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event to check if elytra flight (both through normal and custom elytras) is allowed. All listeners need to return true to allow the entity to fly, otherwise elytra flight will be blocked/stopped

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean useCustomElytra(LivingEntity entity, boolean tickElytra);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event to grant elytra flight to living entities when some condition is met. Will be called when players try to start elytra flight by pressing space in mid-air, and every tick for all flying living entities to check if elytra flight is still allowed. Items that wish to enable custom elytra flight when worn in the chest equipment slot can add the GLIDER component to an item

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: PlayerEntity.SleepFailureReason allowSleep(PlayerEntity player, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that checks whether a player can start to sleep in a bed-like block. This event only applies to sleeping using trySleep(BlockPos). Note: Please use the more detailed events ALLOW_SLEEP_TIME and ALLOW_NEARBY_MONSTERS if they match your use case! This helps with mod compatibility. If this event returns a SleepFailureReason, it is used as the return value of trySleep(BlockPos) and sleeping fails. A null return value means that the player will start sleeping. When this event is called, all vanilla sleeping checks have already succeeded, i.e. this event is used in addition to vanilla checks. The more detailed events ALLOW_SLEEP_TIME and ALLOW_NEARBY_MONSTERS are also checked before this event

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onStartSleeping(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a player can start sleeping in a bed-like block

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onStopSleeping(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when an entity starts to sleep

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: ActionResult allowBed(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, BlockState state, boolean vanillaResult);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when an entity stops sleeping and wakes up

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: ActionResult allowSleepTime(PlayerEntity player, BlockPos sleepingPos, boolean vanillaResult);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a block is a valid bed for the entity. Non-PASS passing return values cancel further callbacks

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: ActionResult allowNearbyMonsters(PlayerEntity player, BlockPos sleepingPos, boolean vanillaResult);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether the current time of day is valid for sleeping. Non-PASS passing return values cancel further callbacks

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowResettingTime(PlayerEntity player);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a player can sleep when monsters are nearby. Non-PASS passing return values cancel further callbacks

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: Direction modifySleepDirection(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, @Nullable Direction sleepingDirection);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a sleeping player counts into skipping the current day and resetting the time to 0

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowSettingSpawn(PlayerEntity player, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Modifies or provides a sleeping direction for a block. The sleeping direction is where a player's head is pointing when they're sleeping

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean setBedOccupationState(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, BlockState bedState, boolean occupied);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a player's spawn can be set when sleeping

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: Vec3d modifyWakeUpPosition(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, BlockState bedState, @Nullable Vec3d wakeUpPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Sets the occupation state of a bed block

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterKilledOtherEntity(ServerWorld world, Entity entity, LivingEntity killedEntity, DamageSource damageSource);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that is called after an entity is directly responsible for killing another entity

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterChangeWorld(Entity originalEntity, Entity newEntity, ServerWorld origin, ServerWorld destination);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event which is called after an entity has been moved to a different world. All entities are copied to the destination and the old entity removed. This event does not apply to the ServerPlayerEntity since players are physically moved to the new world instead of being copied over. A mod may use this event for reference cleanup if it is tracking an entity's current world

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterChangeWorld(ServerPlayerEntity player, ServerWorld origin, ServerWorld destination);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called after an entity has been recreated at the destination when being moved to a different world. Note this event is not called if the entity is a ServerPlayerEntity. AfterPlayerChange should be used to track when a player has changed worlds

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowDamage(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource source, float amount);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that is called when a living entity is going to take damage. This is fired from damage, before armor or any other mitigation are applied. Mods can cancel this to prevent the damage entirely

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterDamage(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource source, float baseDamageTaken, float damageTaken, boolean blocked);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a living entity is going to take damage. Can be used to cancel the damage entirely. The amount corresponds to the "incoming" damage amount, before armor and other mitigations have been applied

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowDeath(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource damageSource, float damageAmount);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called after a living entity took damage, unless they were killed. The base damage taken is given as damage taken before armor or enchantments are applied, but after other effects like shields are applied

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterDeath(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource damageSource);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a living entity takes fatal damage (before totems of undying can take effect)

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onConversion(MobEntity previous, MobEntity converted, EntityConversionContext conversionContext);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a living entity dies. The death cannot be canceled at this point

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void copyFromPlayer(ServerPlayerEntity oldPlayer, ServerPlayerEntity newPlayer, boolean alive);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that is called when the data from an old player is copied to a new player. This event is called after the old player is removed and untracked, but before the new player is added and tracked. Mods may use this event to copy old player data to a new player

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterRespawn(ServerPlayerEntity oldPlayer, ServerPlayerEntity newPlayer, boolean alive);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when player data is copied to a new player

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onJoin(ServerPlayerEntity player);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called after player a has been respawned

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onLeave(ServerPlayerEntity player);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a player has joined the game

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowDeath(ServerPlayerEntity player, DamageSource damageSource, float damageAmount);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Deprecated: Use the more general ALLOW_DEATH event instead and check for instanceof ServerPlayerEntity.

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: void afterBlockBreak(ClientWorld world, ClientPlayerEntity player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback after a block is broken client side. Only called client side. For server side see AFTER

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: boolean onClientPlayerPreAttack(MinecraftClient client, ClientPlayerEntity player, int clickCount);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: This event fires every tick when the attack key (left mouse button by default) is pressed (including clicking and holding the attack key). If the callback returns true, the vanilla handling (block breaking, entity attacking, hand swing) will be cancelled, and the later callbacks of this event are also cancelled. This event is client-only, which means handling it may require sending custom packets. The event fires both when clicking and holding attack key. To check whether the attack key is just clicked, use clickCount != 0 The vanilla attack cooldown and player game mode does not affect this event. The mod probably needs to check attackCooldown and the game mode. ItemCooldownManager can be used for custom item cooldown handling

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ActionResult interact(PlayerEntity player, World world, Hand hand, BlockPos pos, Direction direction);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for left-clicking ("attacking") a block. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! On the logical client, the return values have the following meaning: SUCCESS cancels further processing, causes a hand swing, and sends a packet to the server. CONSUME cancels further processing, and sends a packet to the server. It does NOT cause a hand swing. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server. On the logical server, the return values have the following meaning: PASS falls back to further processing. Any other value cancels further processing

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ActionResult interact(PlayerEntity player, World world, Hand hand, Entity entity, @Nullable EntityHitResult hitResult);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for left-clicking ("attacking") an entity. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! Upon return: SUCCESS cancels further processing and, on the client, sends a packet to the server. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: boolean beforeBlockBreak(World world, PlayerEntity player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, @Nullable BlockEntity blockEntity);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback before a block is broken. Only called on the server, however updates are synced with the client. If any listener cancels a block breaking action, that block breaking action is cancelled and CANCELED event is fired. Otherwise, the AFTER event is fired

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: void afterBlockBreak(World world, PlayerEntity player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, @Nullable BlockEntity blockEntity);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Called before a block is broken and allows cancelling the block breaking. Implementations should not modify the world or assume the block break has completed or failed

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: void onBlockBreakCanceled(World world, PlayerEntity player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, @Nullable BlockEntity blockEntity);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Called after a block is successfully broken

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ItemStack onPickItemFromBlock(ServerPlayerEntity player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, boolean requestIncludeData);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Called when a player requests to pick the item for a block at a given position

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ItemStack onPickItemFromEntity(ServerPlayerEntity player, Entity entity, boolean requestIncludeData);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Determines the pick item stack to give to a player that is attempting to pick an item from a block

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ActionResult interact(PlayerEntity player, World world, Hand hand, BlockHitResult hitResult);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for right-clicking ("using") a block. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! Upon return: SUCCESS cancels further processing and, on the client, sends a packet to the server. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ActionResult interact(PlayerEntity player, World world, Hand hand, Entity entity, @Nullable EntityHitResult hitResult);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for right-clicking ("using") an entity. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! On the logical client, the return values have the following meaning: SUCCESS cancels further processing, causes a hand swing, and sends a packet to the server. CONSUME cancels further processing, and sends a packet to the server. It does NOT cause a hand swing. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server. On the logical server, the return values have the following meaning: PASS falls back to further processing. Any other value cancels further processing. Note that on the server, the EntityHitResult may be null if the client successfully interacted using the interact(Entity, Hand) position-less overload. On the client, the EntityHitResult will never be null

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ActionResult interact(PlayerEntity player, World world, Hand hand);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for right-clicking ("using") an item. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! Upon return: SUCCESS cancels further processing and, on the client, sends a packet to the server. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server

fabric-item-api-v1.function: void getTooltip(ItemStack stack, Item.TooltipContext tooltipContext, TooltipType tooltipType, List<Text> lines);
fabric-item-api-v1.description: Fired after the game has appended all base tooltip lines to the list

fabric-item-api-v1.function: void modify(Predicate<Item> itemPredicate, BiConsumer<ComponentMap.Builder, Item> builderConsumer);
fabric-item-api-v1.description: Modify the default data components of the specified item
Modify the default data components of the specified items

An event that allows overriding whether an Enchantment can be applied to an ItemStack. This should only be used to modify the behavior of external items with regards to external enchantments, where 'external' means either vanilla or from another mod. For instance, a mod might allow enchanting a pickaxe with Sharpness (and only Sharpness) under certain specific conditions. To modify the behavior of your own modded enchantments, specify a custom tag for supportedItems() instead. To modify the behavior of your own modded items, add to the applicable tags instead, when that suffices. Note that this event triggers before canBeEnchantedWith(ItemStack, RegistryEntry, EnchantingContext), and that method will only be called if no listeners override it. Note that allowing an enchantment using this event does not guarantee the item will receive that enchantment, only that it isn't forbidden from doing so
Checks whether an Enchantment should be applied to a given ItemStack

fabric-item-group-api-v1.description: Returns the modify entries event for a specific item group. This uses the group ID and is suitable for modifying a modded item group that might not exist

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called when a BlockEntity is loaded into a ClientWorld. When this event is called, the block entity is already in the world. However, its data might not be loaded yet, so don't rely on it
Called when a BlockEntity is about to be unloaded from a ClientWorld. When this event is called, the block entity is still present on the world
Called when a chunk is loaded into a ClientWorld. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the world
Called when a chunk is about to be unloaded from a ClientWorld. When this event is called, the chunk is still present in the world
Called when an Entity is loaded into a ClientWorld. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the world
Called when an Entity is about to be unloaded from a ClientWorld. This event is called before the entity is unloaded from the world
Called when Minecraft has started and it's client about to tick for the first time. This occurs while the splash screen is displayed
Called when Minecraft's client begins to stop. This is caused by quitting while in game, or closing the game window. This will be called before the integrated server is stopped if it is running
Called at the start of the client tick
Called at the end of the client tick
Called at the start of a ClientWorld's tick
Called at the end of a ClientWorld's tick. End of world tick may be used to start async computations for the next tick
An event which is called after the client world has been changed
Called when tags are loaded or updated
Called when an BlockEntity is loaded into a ServerWorld. When this is event is called, the block entity is already in the world. However, its data might not be loaded yet, so don't rely on it
Called when an BlockEntity is about to be unloaded from a ServerWorld. When this event is called, the block entity is still present on the world

Called when a chunk is loaded into a ServerWorld. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the world. Note that this event is not called for chunks that become accessible without previously being unloaded
Called when a newly generated chunk is loaded into a ServerWorld. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the world

Called when a chunk is unloaded from a ServerWorld. When this event is called, the chunk is still present in the world. Note that the server typically unloads chunks when the chunk's level goes above 45 (and not immediately when the chunk becomes inaccessible). To know when a chunk first becomes inaccessible, see CHUNK_LEVEL_TYPE_CHANGE

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onChunkLevelTypeChange(ServerWorld world, WorldChunk chunk, ChunkLevelType oldLevelType, ChunkLevelType newLevelType);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called when a chunk's actual ticking behavior is about to align with its updated ChunkLevelType. When this event is being called: The chunk's getLevelType() has already changed. Entities within the chunk are not guaranteed to be accessible. The chunk's corresponding level type future in ChunkHolder is not guaranteed to be done. When transitioning from INACCESSIBLE to FULL, calling getChunkFutureSyncOnMainThread(int, int, ChunkStatus, boolean) to fetch the current chunk at FULL status results in undefined behavior
Called when an Entity is loaded into a ServerWorld. When this event is called, the entity is already in the world
Called when an Entity is unloaded from a ServerWorld. This event is called before the entity is removed from the world

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onChange(LivingEntity livingEntity, EquipmentSlot equipmentSlot, ItemStack previousStack, ItemStack currentStack);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called during tick() if the Entity's equipment has been changed or mutated. This event is also called when the entity joins the world. A change in equipment is determined by areEqual(ItemStack, ItemStack)
Called when a Minecraft server is starting. This occurs before the PlayerManager player manager and any worlds are loaded
Called when a Minecraft server has started and is about to tick for the first time. At this stage, all worlds are live

Called when a Minecraft server has started shutting down. This occurs before the server's network channel is closed and before any players are disconnected. For example, an integrated server will begin stopping, but its client may continue to run. All worlds are still present and can be modified

Called when a Minecraft server has stopped. All worlds have been closed and all (block)entities and players have been unloaded. For example, an CLIENT integrated server will begin stopping, but its client may continue to run. Meanwhile, for a SERVER dedicated server, this will be the last event called
Called when a Minecraft server is about to send tag and recipe data to a player

Called right before tags and recipes are sent to a player, either because the player joined, or because the server reloaded resources. The getResourceManager() server resource manager is up-to-date when this is called. For example, this event can be used to sync data loaded with custom resource reloaders

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void endDataPackReload(MinecraftServer server, LifecycledResourceManager resourceManager, boolean success);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called after a Minecraft server has reloaded data packs. If reloading data packs was unsuccessful, the current data packs will be kept

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onBeforeSave(MinecraftServer server, boolean flush, boolean force);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called after data packs on a Minecraft server have been reloaded. If the reload was not successful, the old data packs will be kept

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onAfterSave(MinecraftServer server, boolean flush, boolean force);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called before a Minecraft server begins saving data
Called at the start of the server tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked
Called at the end of the server tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked
Called at the start of a ServerWorld's tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked
Called at the end of a ServerWorld's tick. End of world tick may be used to start async computations for the next tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked
Called just after a world is loaded by a Minecraft server. This can be used to load world specific metadata or initialize a PersistentState on a server world

Called before a world is unloaded by a Minecraft server. This typically occurs after a server has SERVER_STOPPING started shutting down. Mods which allow dynamic world (un)registration should call this event so mods can let go of world handles when a world is removed

fabric-loot-api-v3.function: LootTable replaceLootTable(RegistryKey<LootTable> key, LootTable original, LootTableSource source, RegistryWrapper.WrapperLookup registries);
fabric-loot-api-v3.description: This event can be used to replace loot tables. If a loot table is replaced, the iteration will stop for that loot table

fabric-loot-api-v3.function: void modifyLootTable(RegistryKey<LootTable> key, LootTable.Builder tableBuilder, LootTableSource source, RegistryWrapper.WrapperLookup registries);

fabric-loot-api-v3.description: Called when a loot table is loading to modify loot tables

fabric-loot-api-v3.function: void modifyLootTableDrops(RegistryEntry<LootTable> entry, LootContext context, List<ItemStack> drops);
fabric-loot-api-v3.description: Called when all loot tables have been loaded and REPLACE and MODIFY have been invoked

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowReceiveChatMessage(Text message, @Nullable SignedMessage signedMessage, @Nullable GameProfile sender, MessageType.Parameters params, Instant receptionTimestamp);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: An event triggered when the client receives a chat message, which is any message sent by a player. Mods can use this to block the message. If a listener returned false, the message will not be displayed, the remaining listeners will be called (if any), and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of CHAT
Called when the client receives a chat message, which is any message sent by a player. Returning false prevents the message from being displayed, and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of CHAT

Called when the client receives a game message, which is any message sent by the server. Returning false prevents the message from being displayed, and GAME_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_GAME. Overlay is whether the message will be displayed in the action bar. To toggle overlay, return false and call sendMessage(Text, boolean) ClientPlayerEntity.sendMessage(message, overlay)

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onReceiveChatMessage(Text message, @Nullable SignedMessage signedMessage, @Nullable GameProfile sender, MessageType.Parameters params, Instant receptionTimestamp);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the client receives a game message, which is any message sent by the server. Is not called when ALLOW_GAME game messages are blocked. Use GAME if not modifying the message. Overlay is whether the message will be displayed in the action bar. Use ALLOW_GAME to toggle overlay
Called when the client receives a chat message, which is any message sent by a player. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT chat messages are blocked

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onReceiveChatMessageCanceled(Text message, @Nullable SignedMessage signedMessage, @Nullable GameProfile sender, MessageType.Parameters params, Instant receptionTimestamp);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the client receives a game message, which is any message sent by the server. Is not called when ALLOW_GAME game messages are blocked. Overlay is whether the message will be displayed in the action bar. Use ALLOW_GAME to toggle overlay
Called when receiving a chat message is canceled with ALLOW_CHAT

An event triggered when the client is about to send a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Mods can use this to block the message. If a listener returned false, the message will not be sent, the remaining listeners will not be called (if any), and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_CHAT
Called when the client is about to send a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Returning false prevents the message from being sent, and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_CHAT

Called when the client is about to send a command, which is whenever the player executes a command including client commands registered with fabric-command-api. Returning false prevents the command from being sent, and COMMAND_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_COMMAND. The command string does not include a slash at the beginning
Called when the client sends a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT chat messages are blocked. Use CHAT if not modifying the message

Called when the client sends a command, which is whenever the player executes a command including client commands registered with fabric-command-api. Is not called when ALLOW_COMMAND command messages are blocked. The command string does not include a slash at the beginning. Use COMMAND if not modifying the command
Called when the client sends a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT chat messages are blocked

Called when the client sends a command, which is whenever the player executes a command including client commands registered with fabric-command-api. Is not called when ALLOW_COMMAND command messages are blocked. The command string does not include a slash at the beginning
Called when sending a chat message is canceled with ALLOW_CHAT

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowChatMessage(SignedMessage message, ServerPlayerEntity sender, MessageType.Parameters params);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: An event triggered when the server broadcasts a chat message sent by a player, typically from a client GUI or a player-executed command. Mods can use this to block the message. If a listener returned false, the message will not be broadcast, the remaining listeners will not be called (if any), and CHAT_MESSAGE event will not be triggered. If the message is from a player-executed command, this will be called only if ALLOW_COMMAND_MESSAGE event did not block the message, and after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE event

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowGameMessage(MinecraftServer server, Text message, boolean overlay);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a chat message sent by a player, typically from a client GUI or a player-executed command. Returning false prevents the message from being broadcast and the CHAT_MESSAGE event from triggering. If the message is from a player-executed command, this will be called only if ALLOW_COMMAND_MESSAGE event did not block the message, and after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE event

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowCommandMessage(SignedMessage message, ServerCommandSource source, MessageType.Parameters params);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a game message to all players. Game messages include death messages, join/leave messages, and advancement messages. Returning false prevents the message from being broadcast and the GAME_MESSAGE event from triggering

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onChatMessage(SignedMessage message, ServerPlayerEntity sender, MessageType.Parameters params);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a command message to all players, such as one from /me and /say (but not ones that specify the recipients like /msg). Returning false prevents the message from being broadcast and the COMMAND_MESSAGE event from triggering. If the command is executed by a player and the message is not blocked, ALLOW_CHAT_MESSAGE and CHAT_MESSAGE events will also be triggered after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onGameMessage(MinecraftServer server, Text message, boolean overlay);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a chat message sent by a player, typically from a client GUI or a player-executed command. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT_MESSAGE chat messages are blocked. If the message is from a player-executed command, this will be called only if ALLOW_COMMAND_MESSAGE event did not block the message, and after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE event

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onCommandMessage(SignedMessage message, ServerCommandSource source, MessageType.Parameters params);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a game message to all players. Game messages include death messages, join/leave messages, and advancement messages. Is not called when ALLOW_GAME_MESSAGE game messages are blocked

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ClientConfigurationNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftClient client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client configuration network handler receiving an update indicating the connected server's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ClientConfigurationNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftClient client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client configuration network handler receiving an update indicating the connected server's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ClientPlayNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftClient client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client play network handler receiving an update indicating the connected server's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ClientPlayNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftClient client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client play network handler receiving an update indicating the connected server's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection
Event indicating a connection entering the CONFIGURATION state, ready for registering channel handlers. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
An event called after the connection has been initialized and is ready to start sending and receiving configuration packets. Packets may be sent during this event
An event called after the ReadyS2CPacket has been received, just before switching to the PLAY state. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
An event for the disconnection of the client configuration network handler. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Deprecated: replaced by Complete

Event indicating a connection entered the LOGIN state, ready for registering query request handlers. This event may be used by mods to prepare their client side state. This event does not guarantee that a login attempt will be successful

An event for when the client has started receiving login queries. A client can only start receiving login queries when a server has sent the first login query. Vanilla servers will typically never make the client enter this login phase, but it is not a guarantee that the connected server is a vanilla server since a modded server or proxy may have no login queries to send to the client and therefore bypass the login query phase. If this event is fired then it is a sign that a server is not a vanilla server or the server is behind a proxy which is capable of handling login queries. This event may be used to LoginQueryRequestHandler register login query handlers which may be used to send a response to a server. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
An event for when the client's login process has ended due to disconnection. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Event indicating a connection entered the PLAY state, ready for registering channel handlers

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onPlayReady(ClientPlayNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftClient client);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for notification when the client play network handler is ready to send packets to the server. At this stage, the network handler is ready to send packets to the server. Since the client's local state has been set up
An event for the disconnection of the client play network handler. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked

An event that is called after a player has started tracking an entity. Typically, this occurs when an entity enters a client's view distance. This event is called after the entity's createSpawnPacket spawn packet is sent to the player
Called after a player has started tracking an entity

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ServerConfigurationNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server configuration network handler receiving an update indicating the connected client's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ServerConfigurationNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server configuration network handler receiving an update indicating the connected client's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ServerPlayNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server play network handler receiving an update indicating the connected client's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ServerPlayNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server play network handler receiving an update indicating the connected client's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection
An event for the disconnection of the server configuration network handler. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Event indicating a connection entered the LOGIN state, ready for registering query response handlers

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onLoginStart(ServerLoginNetworkHandler handler, MinecraftServer server, LoginPacketSender sender, ServerLoginNetworking.LoginSynchronizer synchronizer);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the start of login queries of the server login network handler. This event may be used to register LoginQueryResponseHandler login query response handlers using registerReceiver(ServerLoginNetworkHandler, Identifier, ServerLoginNetworking.LoginQueryResponseHandler) since this event is fired just before the first login query response is processed. You may send login queries to the connected client using the provided LoginPacketSender
An event for the disconnection of the server login network handler. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Event indicating a connection entered the PLAY state, ready for registering channel handlers

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onPlayReady(ServerPlayNetworkHandler handler, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for notification when the server play network handler is ready to send packets to the client. At this stage, the network handler is ready to send packets to the client
An event for the disconnection of the server play network handler. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked

fabric-particles-v1.function: boolean allowBlockDustTint(BlockState state, ClientWorld world, BlockPos pos);
fabric-particles-v1.description: An event that checks if a BlockDustParticle block dust particle can be tinted using the corresponding block's BlockColorProvider color provider. The default return value of this event is true. If any callback returns false for a given call, further iteration will be canceled and the event invoker will return false

fabric-rendering-v1.function: void onDrawItemStackOverlay(DrawContext context, TextRenderer textRenderer, ItemStack stack, int x, int y);
fabric-rendering-v1.description: Fires at the end of drawStackOverlay(TextRenderer, ItemStack, int, int, String) and allows for drawing custom item stack decorations. In vanilla these are: durability bar, cooldown overlay and stack count

Called when the world renderer reloads, usually as result of changing resource pack or video configuration, or when the player types F3+A in the debug screen. Afterwards all render chunks will be reset and reloaded. Render chunks and other render-related object instances will be made null or invalid after this event so do not use it to capture dependent state. Instead, use it to invalidate state and reinitialize lazily
An event that can prevent capes from rendering

fabric-rendering-v1.function: void registerRenderers(EntityType<? extends LivingEntity> entityType, LivingEntityRenderer<?, ?, ?> entityRenderer, RegistrationHelper registrationHelper, EntityRendererFactory.Context context);

fabric-rendering-v1.description: Allows registering a mapping from TooltipData to TooltipComponent. This allows custom tooltips for items: first, override getTooltipData and return a custom TooltipData. Second, register a listener to this event and convert the data to your component implementation if it's an instance of your data class. Note that failure to map some data to a component will throw an exception, so make sure that any data you return in getTooltipData will be handled by one of the callbacks

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void beforeInit(MinecraftClient client, Screen screen, int scaledWidth, int scaledHeight);
void afterInit(MinecraftClient client, Screen screen, int scaledWidth, int scaledHeight);

fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called after removed() is called. This event signifies that the screen is now closed. This event is typically used to undo any screen specific state changes or to terminate threads spawned by a screen. This event may precede initialization events BEFORE_INIT but there is no guarantee that event will be called immediately afterwards

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void beforeRender(Screen screen, DrawContext drawContext, int mouseX, int mouseY, float tickDelta);
fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called before a screen is rendered

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void afterBackground(Screen screen, DrawContext drawContext, int mouseX, int mouseY, float tickDelta);
fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called after a screen's background is rendered

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void afterRender(Screen screen, DrawContext drawContext, int mouseX, int mouseY, float tickDelta);
fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called after a screen is rendered
An event that is called before a screen is ticked
An event that is called after a screen is ticked
